title: Render TMDB movies in a responsive grid of shadcn Cards on the homepage
context: Resolve the app root from JOURNAL.md; also resolve the data-layer module path, exported function signature, and chosen filter/sort recorded there by 003-tmdb-data-layer — consume that module, do not re-implement fetching. Per AGENTS.md this Next.js version has breaking changes vs your training data — BEFORE writing any code, read the local docs: node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/12-images.md (next/image + allowing remote hosts), 05-server-and-client-components.md, 06-fetching-data.md, and 08-caching.md (Cache Components); follow their conventions (e.g. how next.config.ts allows remote image hosts in THIS version). ARCHITECTURE: use the Cache Components model — enable `cacheComponents: true` in next.config.ts, cache TMDB data at the data level with the `use cache` directive + `cacheLife`/`cacheTag` in the 003 module (replacing any fetch-level `next: { revalidate }`), and stream the grid behind a `<Suspense>` boundary with a skeleton fallback so the static shell (header + fallback) prerenders. The homepage must stay a server component so the API key never reaches the client. TMDB poster URLs are https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/w500{poster_path}; poster_path can be null, so render a graceful non-broken fallback (e.g. a styled placeholder block). Show vote_average as the 'one additional property' (release year is fine as a secondary detail). SCOPE GUARD: hover effects, CSS transitions, and filter/sort UI are deliberately deferred to tasks 005/006 — do not add them here. Toolchain is Bun.
goal: The homepage server-renders real TMDB movies from the 003 data layer in a responsive Tailwind grid of shadcn Cards using the Cache Components model (cacheComponents enabled, `use cache` data layer, Suspense-wrapped grid with a skeleton loading state), each Card showing a next/image poster (with a graceful fallback when poster_path is null), the title, and the vote_average rating; the production build passes and the grid is verified in the browser at multiple viewport widths.
acceptance: next.config.ts sets `cacheComponents: true`, and `bun run build` reports the `/` route as prerendered (static shell) with the revalidate/expire times from the data layer's cacheLife profile.
The data-layer function in the module recorded in JOURNAL.md is a `use cache` function using `cacheLife`/`cacheTag` from next/cache — no fetch-level `next: { revalidate }` options remain.

src/app/page.tsx contains no 'use client' directive (it remains a server component) and renders the grid inside a `<Suspense>` boundary whose fallback is a skeleton matching the grid layout; the async grid component imports the data-layer function from the 003 module.
No TMDB fetch occurs outside the data layer: `grep -r "themoviedb" src/ --include=*.tsx` returns no matches (the URL lives only in the 003 module).
The grid component imports Card subcomponents from @/components/ui/card and next/image, and renders one Card per movie showing poster, title, and vote_average.

next.config.ts allows the image.tmdb.org remote host using the convention documented in the local next/image docs, and the rendered poster elements on the page are served through the Next.js image optimizer (src contains /_next/image).
The grid container uses responsive Tailwind column classes (e.g. grid-cols-1 with sm:/md:/lg: variants increasing the count).

A null poster_path renders a styled placeholder rather than a broken image (verify via code: the poster render site branches on poster_path; if page 1 has no null case, state that and show the fallback branch).
Run `bun test` from the app root — exits 0 (the test preload must stub next/cache the same way it stubs server-only, since `use cache` is not compiled under bun test).
Run `bun run build` from the app root — exits 0.

Start the server on an open port in the background; via browser tooling open the page and confirm a grid of real movie cards renders with visible posters, titles, and numeric ratings — capture a screenshot as proof; confirm the served HTML contains the prerendered movie content and does NOT contain the TMDB API key or the api.themoviedb.org host.
View the page at a narrow (~375px) and a wide (~1280px) width and confirm the column count differs between them.
Confirm the page loads with no console errors (image host misconfiguration would surface here).
Stop the background server after verification.

JOURNAL.md records the grid/card component file path(s), the extra-property choice (vote_average), and the cache-components decisions (tag name, cacheLife profile, test stub) so tasks 005/006 can build on them.
